title: JEE Main 2026 — 75 Repeated Questions (Part 1)
subject: JEE
source: Eduniti by Mohit Goenka | IIT KGP

questions.text: Two spherical conductors B and C having equal radii and carrying equal charges in them repel each other with a force F when kept apart at some distance. A third spherical conductor having same radius as that of B but uncharged, is brought in contact with B, then brought in contact with C and finally removed away from both. The new force of repulsion between B and C is:
A block of mass m is placed on a smooth fixed sphere of radius R. It slides when pushed slightly. At what vertical distance h, from the top, will it leave the sphere?

Three particles, each of mass M, are situated at the vertices of an equilateral triangle of side length a. The only forces acting on the particles are their mutual gravitational forces. It is desired that each particle moves in a circle while maintaining the original mutual separation a. Find the time period of revolution.

A circular table is rotating with an angular velocity of ω rad/s about its axis (see figure). There is a smooth groove along a radial direction on the table. A steel ball is gently placed at a distance of 1m on the groove. All the surfaces are smooth. If the radius of the table is 3 m, the radial velocity of the ball w.r.t. the table at the time ball leaves the table is x√2ω m/s, where the value of x is……..

A simple pendulum of length 1 m has a wooden bob of mass 1 kg. It is struck by a bullet of mass 10⁻² kg moving with a speed of 2 × 10² m/s. The bullet gets embedded into the bob. The height to which the bob rises before swinging back is: (use g = 10 m/s²)
To get output '1' at R, for the given logic gate circuit, the input values must be:
For the circuit shown, with R₁ = 1.0Ω, R₂ = 2.0Ω, E₁ = 2V and E₂ = E₃ = 4V, the potential difference between the points 'a' and 'b' is approximately (in V):

A capacitor is connected to a 20 V battery through a resistance of 10Ω. It is found that the potential difference across the capacitor rises to 2 V in 1 μs. The capacitance of the capacitor is ……μF. (Given: ln(10/9) = 0.105)

A bullet of mass 0.01 kg and horizontal speed 100 m/s strikes a block of wood of mass 0.49 kg and instantly comes to rest w.r.t. block. The block is suspended from the ceiling by means of thin wires. If the height (in cm) to which the block rises is 2.5p, find the value of p. (take g = 10 m/s²)
A rubber pipe of density 1.5 × 10³ N/m² and Young's modulus 5 × 10⁶ N/m² is suspended from the roof. The length of the pipe is 8 m. What will be the change in length due to its own weight?

On a smooth inclined plane, a body of mass M is attached between two springs. The other ends of the springs are fixed to firm supports. If each spring has force constant K, the period of oscillation of the body (assuming the spring as massless) is:
The interference pattern is obtained with two coherent light sources of intensity ratio 4:1. And the ratio (Imax + Imin)/(Imax − Imin) is 5/x. Then the value of x will be equal to:

Two lighter nuclei combine to form a comparatively heavier nucleus by the relation: ¹₁X + ¹₁X → ²₂Y. The binding energies per nucleon of ¹₁X and ²₂Y are 1.1 MeV and 7.6 MeV respectively. The energy released in this process is _______ MeV.

A person is standing on a weighing machine placed on the floor of an elevator. The motion of the elevator is shown in the adjacent diagram. The maximum and the minimum weights recorded are 66 kg and 57 kg. The true weight of the person is: [Take g = 10 m/s²]

In an experiment to find out the diameter of wire using screw gauge, the following observations were noted: (a) Screw moves 0.5 mm on main scale in one complete rotation (b) Total divisions on circular scale = 50 (c) Main scale reading is 2.5 mm (d) 45th division of circular scale is in the pitch line (e) Instrument has 0.03 mm negative error. Then the diameter of wire is:
A body is projected from the ground at an angle of 45° with the horizontal. Its velocity after 2s is 20 m/s. The maximum height reached by the body during its motion is ______ m. (use g = 10 m/s²)

A capacitor C is fully charged with voltage V₀. After disconnecting the voltage source, it is connected in parallel with another uncharged capacitor of capacitance C/2. The energy loss in the process after the charge is distributed between the two capacitors is:

A simple pendulum is being used to determine the value of gravitational acceleration g at a certain place. The length of the pendulum is 25.0 cm and a stop watch with 1 s resolution measures the time taken for 40 oscillations to be 50 s. The accuracy in g is:

A spherically symmetric charge distribution is considered with charge density varying as ρ(r) = ρ₀(3/4 − r/R) for r ≤ R and Zero for r > R. Where r(r < R) is the distance from the centre O (as shown in figure). The electric field at point P will be:

The value of the acceleration due to gravity is g₁ at a height h = R/2 (R = radius of the earth) from the surface of the earth. It is again equal to g₁ at a depth d below the surface of the earth. The ratio (d/R) equals:

A Zener of breakdown voltage VZ = 8V and maximum zener current IZM = 10 mA is subjected to an input voltage Vi = 10V with series resistance R = 100Ω. In the given circuit RL represents the variable load resistance. The ratio of maximum and minimum value of RL is:

Consider a rectangular sheet of solid material of length ℓ = 9 cm and width d = 4 cm. The coefficient of linear expansion is α = 3.1 × 10⁻⁵ K⁻¹ at room temperature and one atmospheric pressure. The mass of sheet m = 0.1 kg and the specific heat capacity Cv = 900 J kg⁻¹K⁻¹. If the amount of heat supplied to the material is 8.1 × 10² J then change in area of the rectangular sheet is:
The centre of mass of a thin rectangular plate (fig-x) with sides of length a and b, whose mass per unit area (σ) varies as σ = σ₀x/ab (where σ₀ is a constant), would be:

A bead of mass 'm' slides without friction on the wall of a vertical circular hoop of radius 'R'. The bead moves under the combined action of gravity and a massless spring (k) attached to the bottom of the hoop. The equilibrium length of the spring is 'R'. If the bead is released from top of the hoop with zero initial speed, velocity of bead when the length of spring becomes 'R', would be (spring constant is 'k', g is acceleration due to gravity):

A steel wire of length 2 m and Young's modulus 2.0 × 10¹¹ Nm⁻² is stretched by a force. If Poisson ratio and transverse strain for the wire are 0.2 and 10⁻³ respectively, then the elastic potential energy density of the wire is _____ × 10⁵ (in SI units)

In the experiment for measurement of viscosity 'η' of given liquid with a ball having radius R, consider following statements:
A. Graph between terminal velocity V and R will be a parabola
B. The terminal velocities of different diameter balls are constant for a given liquid.
C. Measurement of terminal velocity is dependent on the temperature.
D. This experiment can be utilized to assess the density of a given liquid.
E. If balls are dropped with some initial speed, the value of η will change.
Choose the correct answer:

Two slabs with square cross section of different materials (1, 2) with equal sides (l) and thickness d₁ and d₂ such that d₂ = 2d₁ and l > d₂. Lower edges fixed to the floor. Equal shearing force applied on narrow faces. Angle of deformation θ₂ = 2θ₁. If shear moduli of material 1 is 4 × 10⁹ N/m², then shear moduli of material 2 is x × 10⁹ N/m², where x is:

Consider a completely full cylindrical water tank of height 1.6 m and cross-sectional area 0.5 m². It has a small hole in its side at a height 90 cm from the bottom. A load 50 kg is applied at the top surface of the water in the tank. The velocity of the water coming out at the instant when the hole is opened is: (g = 10 m/s²)

Two simple pendulums having lengths l₁ and l₂ with negligible string mass undergo angular displacements θ₁ and θ₂ from their mean positions. If the angular accelerations of both pendulums are same, then which expression is correct?

An ideal gas exists in a state with pressure P₀, volume V₀. It is isothermally expanded to 4 times its initial volume, then isobarically compressed to its original volume. Finally the system is heated isochorically to bring it to its initial state. The amount of heat exchanged in this process is:

A sinusoidal wave of wavelength 7.5 cm travels a distance of 1.2 cm along the x-direction in 0.3 sec. The crest P is at x = 0 at t = 0 sec and maximum displacement of the wave is 2 cm. Which equation correctly represents this wave?

An electron is made to enter symmetrically between two parallel and equally but oppositely charged metal plates, each of 10 cm length. The electron emerges with horizontal component of velocity 10⁶ m/s. If the magnitude of the electric field between the plates is 9.1 V/cm, the vertical component of velocity of electron is:
(mass of electron = 9.1 × 10⁻³¹ kg, charge = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C)

A coil of area A and N turns is rotating with angular velocity ω in a uniform magnetic field B about an axis perpendicular to B. Magnetic flux φ and induced emf ε across it, at an instant when B is parallel to the plane of coil, are:
An infinite wire has a circular bend of radius a, and carrying a current I as shown in figure. The magnitude of magnetic field at the origin O of the arc is given by:

In the first configuration (1), four identical charges (q₀) are kept at the corners A, B, C and D of a square of side length 'a'. In the second configuration (2), the same charges are shifted to mid points G, E, H and F. If K = 1/4πε₀, the difference between the potential energies of configuration (2) and (1) is given by:

Consider a long straight wire of circular cross-section (radius a) carrying a steady current I uniformly distributed across its cross-section. The distances from the centre at which the magnetic field [inside the wire, outside the wire] is half of the maximum possible magnetic field, are:

A small bob of mass 100 mg and charge +10 μC is connected to an insulating string of length 1 m. It is brought near an infinitely long non-conducting sheet of charge density 'σ'. If string subtends an angle of 45° with the sheet at equilibrium, the charge density of sheet will be:
(Given, ε₀ = 8.85 × 10⁻¹² F/m and g = 10 m/s²)

A dipole with two electric charges of 2 μC magnitude each, with separation distance 0.5 μm, is placed between the plates of a capacitor such that its axis is parallel to the electric field when potential difference of 5 V is applied. Separation between the plates is 0.5 mm. If the dipole is rotated by 30° from the axis, the value of torque is:

In the figure shown, a resistance of 150.4 Ω is connected in series to an ammeter A of resistance 240 Ω. A shunt resistance of 10 Ω is connected in parallel with the ammeter. Battery = 20V. The reading of the ammeter is _____ mA.

An infinitely long wire has uniform linear charge density λ = 2 nC/m. The net flux through a Gaussian cube of side length √3 cm, if the wire passes through any two corners maximally displaced, would be xNm²C⁻¹, where x is:

Two cells of emf 1V and 2V and internal resistance 2Ω and 1Ω, respectively, are connected in series with an external resistance of 6Ω. Total current is I₁. Same two cells in parallel are connected to same external resistance; total current drawn is I₂. The value of (I₁/I₂) is x/3. The value of x is:

A tightly wound long solenoid carries a current of 1.5 A. An electron is executing uniform circular motion inside the solenoid with a time period of 75 ns. The number of turns per metre in the solenoid is _______.
[Take me = 9 × 10⁻³¹ kg, |qe| = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C, μ₀ = 4π × 10⁻⁷ N/A², 1 ns = 10⁻⁹ s]

In a moving coil galvanometer, two moving coils M₁ and M₂ have the following particulars:
R₁=5Ω, N₁=15, A₁=3.6×10⁻³ m², B₁=0.25T
R₂=7Ω, N₂=21, A₂=1.8×10⁻³ m², B₂=0.50T
Assuming same torsional constant of springs, the ratio of voltage sensitivity of M₁ and M₂ is:

The entropy of any system is given by S = α²β ln[μkR/Jβ² + 3], where α and β are constants, μ, J, k and R are no. of moles, mechanical equivalent of heat, Boltzmann constant and gas constant. [Take S = dQ/T]
Choose the incorrect option:
opts: α and J have the same dimensions.
S, β, k and μR have the same dimensions.
S and α have different dimensions.
α and k have the same dimensions.

questions.text: Water droplets are coming from an open tap at a particular rate. The spacing between a droplet observed at 4th second after its fall to the next droplet is 34.3 m. At what rate are the droplets coming from the tap? (Take g = 9.8 m/s²)

The boxes of masses 2 kg and 8 kg are connected by a massless string passing over smooth pulleys. The 8 kg box is 20 cm above the ground. Calculate the time taken by box of mass 8 kg to strike the ground starting from rest. (use g = 10 m/s²)

Given is the plot of a potential energy function U(x) for a system in which a particle is in one dimensional motion. Suppose that Emech = 8 J. The incorrect statement for this system is:
(1) at x > x₄, K.E. is constant throughout the region.
(2) at x < x₁, K.E. is smallest and the particle is moving at the slowest speed.
(3) at x = x₂, K.E. is greatest and the particle is moving at the fastest speed.
(4) at x = x₃, K.E. = 4 J.

A particle of mass 1 mg and charge q is lying at the mid-point of two stationary particles kept at a distance '2 m' when each is carrying same charge 'q'. If the free charged particle is displaced through distance 'x' (x << 1 m), it executes SHM. Its angular frequency of oscillation will be _____ × 10⁵ rad/s if q² = 10 C².

Due to cold weather a 1 m water pipe of cross-sectional area 1 cm² is filled with ice at −10°C. Resistive heating (0.5 A through 4 kΩ resistance) is used to melt it. What is the minimum time required?
(Latent heat of fusion = 3.33 × 10⁵ J/kg, specific heat of ice = 2 × 10³ J/kg, density of ice = 10³ kg/m³)

The image of an object placed in air formed by a convex refracting surface is at a distance of 10 m behind the surface. The image is real and is at 2/3 of the distance of the object from the surface. The wavelength of light inside the surface is 2/3 times the wavelength in air. The radius of the curved surface is x/13 m. The value of x is:

The electric field in an electromagnetic wave is given by E = (50 NC⁻¹) sinω(t – x/c). The energy contained in a cylinder of volume V is 5.5 × 10⁻¹² J. The value of V is _____ cm³.
(given ε₀ = 8.8 × 10⁻¹² C²N⁻¹m⁻²)

A glass tumbler having inner depth of 17.5 cm is kept on a table. A student starts pouring water (μ = 4/3) into it while looking from above. When he feels the tumbler is half filled, he stops. Up to what height is the tumbler actually filled?
Electric field of plane electromagnetic wave propagating through a non-magnetic medium is given by E = 20cos(2 × 10¹⁰ t – 200x) V/m. The dielectric constant of the medium is equal to: (Take μᵣ = 1)

Consider a uniform rod of mass M = 4m and length l pivoted about its centre. A mass m moving with velocity v making angle θ = π/4 to the rod's long axis collides with one end and sticks to it. The angular speed of the rod-mass system just after the collision is:

In the figure shown, the current in the 10V battery is close to:
(Circuit: 5Ω–10Ω parallel branch with 10V; 20V–2Ω–4Ω combination)
opts: 0.36 A from negative to positive terminal
0.71 A from positive to negative terminal
0.21 A from positive to negative terminal
0.42 A from positive to negative terminal

questions.text: In a meter bridge experiment, S is a standard resistance. R is a resistance wire. Balancing length is l = 25 cm. If R is replaced by a wire of half length and half diameter of same material, the balancing distance l (in cm) will now be:

A part of a complete circuit is shown. At some instant, current I = 1 A and decreasing at rate 10² As⁻¹. The value of potential difference Vₚ – Vₒ (in volts) is:
(L = 50mH, R = 2Ω, Battery = 30V)

An air bubble of volume 1 cm³ rises from the bottom of a lake 40m deep to the surface at temperature 12°C. Atmospheric pressure = 1 × 10⁵ Pa, density of water = 1000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s². The volume of air bubble when it reaches the surface will be:

1 kg of water at 100°C is converted into steam at 100°C by boiling at atmospheric pressure. Volume changes from 1.00 × 10⁻³ m³ to 1.671 m³. The change in internal energy of the system will be:
(Latent heat = 2257 kJ/kg, Atmospheric pressure = 1 × 10⁵ Pa)

A cubical volume is bounded by x = 0, x = a, y = 0, y = a, z = 0, z = a. Electric field E⃗ = E₀x î, where E₀ = 4 × 10⁴ NC⁻¹m⁻¹. If a = 2 cm, the charge contained in the cubical volume is Q × 10⁻¹⁴ C. The value of Q is:
(Take ε₀ = 9 × 10⁻¹² C²/Nm²)

The mean free path of molecules of a certain gas at STP is 1500d, where d is the diameter of the gas molecules. While maintaining the standard pressure, the mean free path of the molecules at 373 K is approximately:

Two long parallel wires carrying currents 8A and 15A in opposite directions are placed at a distance of 7 cm from each other. A point P is equidistant from both wires such that the lines joining P to the wires are perpendicular to each other. The magnitude of magnetic field at P is _____ × 10⁻⁶ T. (Given: √2 = 1.4)

For designing a voltmeter of range 50 V and an ammeter of range 10 mA using a galvanometer (coil resistance 54 Ω, full scale deflection for 1 mA). Choose the correct answer:
(A) for voltmeter R ≈ 50 kΩ (B) for ammeter r ≈ 0.2 Ω (C) for ammeter r ≈ 6 Ω (D) for voltmeter R ≈ 5 kΩ (E) for voltmeter R ≈ 500 Ω

The current sensitivity of moving coil galvanometer is increased by 25%, achieved only by changing number of turns and area while keeping resistance constant. The percentage change in the voltage sensitivity will be:

An ideal transformer with purely resistive load operates at 12 kV on primary side. It supplies electrical energy at 120 V. The average rate of energy consumption is 60 kW. The value of resistive load (Rs) required in the secondary circuit will be _____ mΩ.

In Young's double slit experiment, a thin plate of thickness t = 10 μm and refractive index μ = 1.2 is inserted in front of slit S₁. The experiment uses monochromatic light of wavelength λ = 500 nm. Due to the plate insertion, central maxima is shifted by xβ₀ (β₀ = fringe-width before insertion). The value of x is:

In a Young's double slit experiment, the intensities at two points, for the path differences λ/4 and λ/3 (λ = wavelength) are I₁ and I₂ respectively. If I₀ = intensity produced by each individual slit, then (I₁ + I₂)/I₀ = ……

Unpolarised light of intensity 32 Wm⁻² passes through three polaroids such that the pass axis of the last polaroid is perpendicular to that of the first polaroid. If intensity of emerging light is 3 Wm⁻², the angle between pass axis of first two polaroids is _____ °

The output Y for inputs A and B of the circuit (AND gate feeding a NOR gate with B). The truth table is:
(A=0,B=0)→Y=0, (A=0,B=1)→Y=1, (A=1,B=0)→Y=1, (A=1,B=1)→Y=0
If each diode has a forward bias resistance of 25 Ω in the below circuit (D₁, D₂, D₃ in parallel with 125Ω each; 25Ω at bottom; 5V source), which option is correct:

A monochromatic light is incident on a hydrogen sample in ground state. Hydrogen atoms absorb a fraction of light and subsequently emit radiation of six different wavelengths. The frequency of incident light is x × 10¹⁵ Hz. The value of x is:
(Given h = 4.25 × 10⁻¹⁵ eVs)
A sphere of relative density σ and diameter D has concentric cavity of diameter d. The ratio D/d, if it just floats on water in a tank is:

A parallel plate capacitor with plate separation 5 mm is charged. On introducing a dielectric sheet of thickness 2 mm, while keeping battery connections intact, the capacitor draws 25% more charge from the battery. The dielectric constant of the sheet is:
The effective resistance between A and B, if resistance of each resistor is R, will be: (Wheatstone bridge / diamond ladder network)

Identify the characteristics of an adiabatic process in a monoatomic gas:
(A) Internal energy is constant.
(B) Work done in the process is equal to the change in internal energy.
(C) The product of temperature and volume is a constant.
(D) The product of pressure and volume is a constant.
(E) The work done to change the temperature from T₁ to T₂ is proportional to (T₂ – T₁)
Choose the correct answer:
